title: V interviews Tiffany Grant
wikitext:
[]: V interviews Tiffany Grant Post by BrikHaus » Wed Apr 21, 2010 3:17 pm [url]http://snausages.org/tiffany-grant-interview-with-reva/[/url] Our (least) favorite Evangelion fan, V, has recently uploaded a multi-part interview with Tiffany Grant. It's horrible, and I was unable to watch the entire thing, especially because he interviews her as V, in full costume. But anyway, I thought fans of the dub might want to see this. One somewhat interesting point: The one point that Matt Greenfield, ADV’s ADR director for Evangelion, really put his foot down on with Gainax, was the “Children” plural thing. In the original Japanese, they use the plural word “Children”; Shinji is the “Third Children”, Asuka is the “Second Children”. Greenfield insisted that they change this to “Third Child” etc., singular, because it just sounds really weird in English. I asked Tiffany point-blank, “Did Gainax ever say that the fact that its pluralized as “Children” is a reference to something? Does it have any actual meaning?” She said they *never* said it meant anything, its just that Japanese has no pluralization, so they though “Children” just “sounded cool”…and Greenfield really had to fight with them to say that unless this was actually referring to something (they never said it did), it sounds like such bad English that it wouldn’t “sound cool” in the English market, and just be jarring.

[]: Post by Joseph the PRPD » Wed Apr 21, 2010 3:19 pm I wish this was a joke. EDIT: ns;rovninfdsobsaie4fzamlvdppp EDITX2 : Some parts are pretty interesting.

It's horrible, and I was unable to watch the entire thing, especially because he interviews her as V, in full costume. But anyway, I thought fans of the dub might want to see this. One somewhat interesting point:

[]: Post by Seele00TextOnly » Wed Apr 21, 2010 3:26 pm Have to agree with Greenfield on that one. I would be surprised if there actually was any 'fighting' to get that done though. Considering the other changes they allowed such as the new German dialogue, of course they wouldn't care about this trivial yet almost necessary alteration. EDIT: because herd mentality

[]: Post by NAveryW » Wed Apr 21, 2010 3:39 pm Haven't listened yet, but 2015: The Last Year of Ryoji Kaji did a pretty good job of insinuating that there's an actual reason for use of the word "Children". I'll probably edit my post after I finish listening. That's right: not watching. Listening.

[]: Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Apr 21, 2010 3:42 pm NAveryW wrote: That's right: not watching. Listening.

[]: Post by Joseph the PRPD » Wed Apr 21, 2010 3:45 pm I can't watch this anymore. The audio quality sucks, the video quality sucks, and I can't take V seriously in that stupid get up. This is just painful to watch or listen. V's voice is annoying. The Eva Monkey wrote: Not to mention it looks like he ambushed her during her lunch.

[]: Post by J_Faulkner » Wed Apr 21, 2010 3:50 pm Props to V for getting out there and being proactive. I may not like his analyses or his approach, but at least he shows the desire and hunger to disseminate Evangelion to the masses. For that, I can only applaud him.

[]: Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Apr 21, 2010 4:01 pm PRPD wrote: UGH wrote: I can only applaud him.

[]: Post by Ornette » Wed Apr 21, 2010 4:06 pm NAveryW wrote: Haven't listened yet, but 2015: The Last Year of Ryoji Kaji did a pretty good job of insinuating that there's an actual reason for use of the word "Children". I'll probably edit my post after I finish listening.

[]: Post by NAveryW » Wed Apr 21, 2010 4:08 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Not to mention it looks like he ambushed her during her lunch.

[]: Post by J_Faulkner » Wed Apr 21, 2010 4:14 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: He is the Fox News of Evangelion fandom.

[]: Post by ran1 » Wed Apr 21, 2010 4:53 pm Che Guevara of Evangelion fandom.

[]: Post by Sachi » Wed Apr 21, 2010 4:56 pm Faulkner: You should get to know V better. I'm not saying you won't like him, but you should at least talk to him. Join his forums or something.

[]: Post by skikes » Wed Apr 21, 2010 5:23 pm The link isn't working for me. Who is this V?

[]: Post by ran1 » Wed Apr 21, 2010 5:25 pm Just google "revolution of eva". Its the first one. Also, inb4ZapXblock because we're talking about V.

[]: Post by BobBQ » Wed Apr 21, 2010 5:27 pm J_Faulkner wrote: Perhaps we can say he's the Che Guevara of Evangelion fandom.

[]: Post by ran1 » Wed Apr 21, 2010 5:32 pm BobBQ wrote: No. Che at least had the redeeming virtue of looking cool on a t-shirt.

[]: Post by skikes » Wed Apr 21, 2010 5:39 pm ran1 wrote: Just google "revolution of eva". Its the first one. Also, inb4ZapXblock because we're talking about V.

[]: Post by UrsusArctos » Wed Apr 21, 2010 5:45 pm You should be able to find it easily enough. We don't link to that site here, for good reason.
